module_mood_contract: mechanical rescue, not triumph — 'ahh, this is actually fixable,' with M3 strategic uncertainty and M4 security residual still alive; close-of-M5 takeaway is the portable judge-builder, not 'quality is solved'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named — every module has a deliberate mood; mood-floor 8+/10 on teaching beats.
Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood. Unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L5: 'Self-consistency asks a different question: if the agent writes the briefing again from the same evidence, what stays stable and what drifts?' — positive discipline-framed opener.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — don't front-run a downstream teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L13 names M6's eval loop only as the production-scale destination ('the kind Module 6's eval loop wires up') without teaching it. L66 'have a multi-method judge ready' stays at M5's level — judge as artefact, not yet judge-in-a-loop. M6's automation + correction-feedback beat is preserved.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Placed after the Hallucination benchmark exercise (output-quality.md L34). Names the pattern just lived through (scoreboard rescued groundedness; here's a different shape of uncertainty the scoreboard didn't measure). Closer placement correct.
This IS the closer; rule applies to openers.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Forcing functions are encoded in the prompts (Prompt 1: subagent MUST NOT read briefing/claim-pool/scoreboard — preserves blind regeneration; 'do not summarize the briefing in chat' — prevents leakage; Prompt 2's structured five-section comparison; Prompt 3's 'ask me what output, where evidence lives, then write the judge file under judges/'). Body prose teaches the contrast (groundedness vs reproducibility) without smuggling the move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt 2 keeps the comparison in chat scrollback (no file write). Maintainer block L93 explicitly notes self-study students 'may run both prompts and read the comparison in chat scrollback (or ask Claude to save it to a file if they want to keep it).' Optional, not proof-of-reflection.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with neighbor / discuss with the room / pause and compare' authored conversation cues. L11 'The trainer can demo this live. Students can run the prompts too' describes delivery mode (per training-architecture conventions), not a cohort-management instruction. Body remains about the work.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Host-skill is optional; default cohort is no host-skill.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block describes the trainer demo path and a self-study path; no host-skill prerequisite or universal-prework framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teach the principle and the technique — default; wow-mood carve-out.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body explains WHY self-consistency contrasts with groundedness (stability ≠ truth), names what it can and cannot prove (L7, L63), and Prompt 3 produces an explainable judge file with a 'Known limit:' line. Black-box invocation absent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt 3's quality-control judge asks the student to bring (a) what output, (b) where evidence lives, (c) short filename — student owns the surfacing, Claude authors the judge artifact. Correct division.

L12 explicitly marks the student-action as optional ('Students can run the prompts too, but they do not need the artifact to complete Module 5'). L93 maintainer note formalizes optionality. No yoga-instructor pacing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reading burden for verification is banned — Claude audits Claude.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompt 2 has Claude do the cross-briefing comparison (stable / drifted / first-only / second-only / self-consistency issues) — student reads the report in chat, but the diffing/spotting work is Claude's. Reading the comparison is for emotional/judgement payoff, not error-catching.
Trace mood_score 8 across the single beat; close 8. Lecture sits in M5's mood-arc-carrying half (rescue beat), refines but does not steal the exercise's 9-design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: M5 rescues groundedness only — does NOT resolve M3 strategic uncertainty or M4 security residual.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L7 'A claim can be stable and still unsupported' and L9 'self-consistency sits after the scoring' explicitly bound the rescue. L63 'Whether the winning groundedness judge should change. Default answer should be no unless the demo shows a concrete failure class the judge can actually detect' resists the temptation to demote groundedness in favor of a more mysterious check. M3/M4 residuals stay where they are.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Take-home pivots from 'rerun the benchmark' to 'have a multi-method judge ready for outputs you ship.'
rules_evaluated.evidence: L66 'The take-home move is not "always run self-consistency." The take-home move is: have a multi-method judge ready for outputs you ship.' L67 generalises target ('a customer email, a pricing memo, a positioning draft, anything you'd want a check on before it goes out'). Prompt 3 enacts the take-home as a portable judge-builder. The functionally-lighter take-home (per the orchestrator's framing — judge-builder, not benchmark-rerun) lands cleanly because the M5 exercise already taught empirical method selection; the lecture inherits that competence and ports it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Hand-off to M6 is hunger, not closure.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M5→M6 seam ('30 claims here, hundreds on every build') lives primarily in the EXERCISE close per strategy doc; this lecture's hand-off is portability of the multi-method shape. L13 'For a production check (the kind Module 6's eval loop wires up), you'd want N=20 to 30' nods forward without front-running. The exercise close + lecture close together produce hunger; lecture does not steal that beat.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch (every forcing function names dominant failure + recovery).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture file with single phase and optional student-action; failure-mode rows live in the M5 exercise maintainer (output-quality.md L293 'student treats self-consistency as proof' — caught and named). Adjacent doc carries the escape hatch.
